mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate directly lowers the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with monthly FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.5 percent, labor market deterioration confirms a recessionary shift, causing institutional investors to reduce equity allocations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 5.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in housing starts and real retail sales indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and housing demand, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline of HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict negative 60-day returns with a directional accuracy above 55 percent over a 24-month out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production during periods of elevated inflation signals stagflationary supply shocks, which compress corporate margins and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 215 and INDPRO declines, S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 2.5 percent indicates aggressive central bank intervention to stabilize financial markets, which reduces tail-risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 65 percent of trading days following a FEDFUNDS reading below 2.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payrolls reflects immediate corporate cost-cutting and weakening economic activity, which directly translates to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS MoM changes against 20-day forward returns remains below 0.01, or directional accuracy does not exceed 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly inflation, where GDP decelerates while CPI rises, confirms a demand-pull inflation trap that forces monetary tightening expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-CPIAUCSL divergence signal yields a negative Rank IC over any 24-month rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in over 60 percent of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: When the index of industrial production falls below 110, manufacturing capacity utilization drops sharply, signaling a broad supply-side contraction that reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 65 percent of months where INDPRO trades below 110.
